canksik/;k;] la;qDr lfpo MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th March, 2014 S.O. 964(E).—Whereas, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highway Act 1956 (48 of 1956) read with the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008, the Central Government by notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways published vide S.O. No. 2980 (E) dated the 21st December, 2012, authorized National Highways Authority of India (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”) for levying and collecting fee on mechanical vehicles for the use of the bridges constructed across river Narmada at Zadeshwar in the years 1997 & 2000 in the area from km 192.000 to km 198.000 of National Highway No. 8 (herein after referred to as the “said section”) at the rates and category of vehicles specified in its schedule. And whereas, pursuant to the provision of section-14 of the National Highways Authority of India Act (68 of 1988), the Authority entered into an Agreement on 23rd July, 2012 (hereinafter referred to as said “Agreement”) with M/s Narmada Bridge Tollway Limited, Mumbai (hereinafter referred to as the “Concessionaire”) for the project of Six laning of kilometer 192.000 to kilometer 198.000 between Vadodara-Surat Section of NH–8 i.e. said section including construction of new four lane extra Dozed Bridge across river Narmada in the State of Gujarat on DBFOT (Toll) basis under Phase NHDP-V; And whereas, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highway Act 1956 (48 of 1956) read with the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008, the Central Government by notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways published vide S.O. No. 485 (E) dated 27th February, 2013 (superseding the notification bearing S.O. No. 2980 (E) dated 21st December, 2012) authorized the Concessionaire for levying and collecting fee on mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section on and from the Date of Commercial Operation till the termination date. And whereas, a Memorandum of Understanding was entered between the Authority and the Concessionaire on 17th July, 2013 for rehabilitation work of Existing Narmada Bridge and subsequent foreclosure of the Agreement dated 23rd July, 2012. And whereas, the Authority was requested by Ministry of Road Transport and Highways vide letter No. H-39011/32/2013-P&P (Toll) dated 20th January, 2014 for taking action for issuing the notification for stopping the levying of user fee on old bridges on River Narmada. And whereas, the Date of Commercial Operation was not declared and the Agreement dated 23rd July, 2012 has been foreclosed by the Authority on 7th February, 2014 on mutual consent basis. Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 as amended from time to time, the Central Government hereby rescinds the notification bearing S.O. No. 485 (E) dated 27th February, 2013. [F. No. NHAI/13013/722/12-13/CO/GC (Vadodara-Surat) BOT] SANJAY BANDOPADHYAYA, Jt. Secy.
